description: Fix two documentation inaccuracies found during a systematic audit of all 24 docs pages against the source code: (1) a config.json schema field that does not exist in the actual config interface, and (2) an incorrect explanation of TypeScript's noImplicitOverride behavior with code examples that contradict the official scaffold output.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove incorrect port field from config schema table
userStories.description: As a user reading the configuration reference, I want the config.json schema to only document fields that actually exist in the OpentabsConfig interface, so that I don't set a port field that silently does nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: The `port` row is removed from the config schema table in configuration.mdx (the table row at approximately lines 57-62 that documents port as a number field with default 9515)
No other reference to `port` as a config.json field remains on the page (the environment variables section documenting OPENTABS_PORT and PORT is correct and must remain)
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter date is updated to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx

The config schema table (starting around line 35) lists a `port` field at approximately lines 57-62:
```
<Table.Row>
  <Table.Cell className="font-medium">`port`</Table.Cell>
  <Table.Cell className="*:text-xs">`number`</Table.Cell>
  <Table.Cell className="*:text-xs">_(omitted — uses default 9515)_</Table.Cell>
  <Table.Cell>MCP server port. When set to the default value (`9515`), the field is removed from config.json rather than stored explicitly.</Table.Cell>
</Table.Row>
```

This field does NOT exist in the OpentabsConfig interface in platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts (lines 40-51). The interface only has: localPlugins, tools, browserToolPolicy, permissions, skipConfirmation.

The port is resolved by platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts via: CLI --port flag > OPENTABS_PORT env var > DEFAULT_PORT (9515). It is never read from config.json.

Remove only the `port` table row. Do NOT remove the environment variables section (around line 185+) that documents OPENTABS_PORT and PORT — those are correct.

Also check the example config JSON block (around line 68) — if it includes a `port` field, remove it from the example too.

userStories.title: Fix incorrect override explanation and code examples in resources-prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the resources and prompts guide, I want accurate TypeScript guidance so that I understand when the override keyword is actually required.
acceptanceCriteria: The explanation paragraph (approximately line 158) no longer claims that override is required for ALL inherited class members — it correctly states that override is needed for optional (non-abstract) inherited members like `resources` and `prompts`, but not for abstract members like `name`, `version`, `description`, etc.
The code example at approximately lines 151-155 removes `override` from the `name` property (which is abstract) while keeping `override` on the `resources` property (which is non-abstract optional)

The full plugin example at approximately lines 494-507 removes `override` from abstract properties (`name`, `version`, `description`, `urlPatterns`, `tools`) while keeping `override` on non-abstract optional properties (`resources`, `prompts`) and on `isReady()` — matching the convention used by the official scaffold in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 354-359
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter date is updated to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

Three locations need fixing:

1. The explanation paragraph at line 158 currently says:
"`override` is required because TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule (enabled in the project's tsconfig) requires the keyword when overriding any inherited class member. Omitting `override` causes a TS4114 type error at build time."

This is WRONG for abstract members. TypeScript's noImplicitOverride only requires `override` for non-abstract inherited members. The scaffold code (platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 354-359) confirms this — it generates `readonly name = '...'` WITHOUT override for abstract properties, and the scaffolded code compiles fine with noImplicitOverride: true.

Fix the explanation to say something like: "`override` is required on the `resources` property because it has a default value in the base class and TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule requires the keyword when overriding non-abstract inherited members. Abstract properties like `name` do not require `override`."

2. The code example at lines 151-155:
```typescript
class MyPlugin extends OpenTabsPlugin {
  override readonly name = 'my-plugin';        // Remove override (abstract)
  override readonly resources: ResourceDefinition[] = [channelList, userProfile];  // Keep override (non-abstract)
}
```

3. The full example at lines 494-507 uses `override` on everything. Fix to match scaffold convention:
- `readonly name = 'tracker'` (no override — abstract)
- `readonly version = '1.0.0'` (no override — abstract)
- `readonly displayName = 'Project Tracker'` (no override — abstract)
- `readonly description = 'Manage project items'` (no override — abstract)
- `readonly urlPatterns = [...]` (no override — abstract)
- `readonly tools: ToolDefinition[] = [...]` (no override — abstract)
- `override readonly resources: ResourceDefinition[] = [...]` (keep override — non-abstract optional)
- `override readonly prompts: PromptDefinition[] = [...]` (keep override — non-abstract optional)
- `override async isReady()` — keep override, as isReady() is abstract but the scaffold also uses override for method implementations (this is fine either way)

Reference: platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts lines 190-222 shows which properties are abstract vs optional.
Reference: platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 353-359 shows the convention the scaffold uses.
